title: Change entrypoint parameter behaviour to match docker Fixes #564
body:
Behaviour updated to match docker.

1. container entrypoint is no longer overwritten by user command and command is instead passed as parameter to entrypoint
2. container cmd is overwritten by user command (existing behaviour)

Signed-off-by: PixelDrift Consulting <support@pixeldrift.net>

comments.body:
Behaviour comparison:

When `entrypoint` is set for container:
~~~
buildah run buildah-ep param1 param2
All: param1 param2
1: param1
2: param2

docker run buildah:ep param1 param2
All: param1 param2
1: param1
2: param2
~~~
  
When `cmd` is set for container:
~~~
buildah run buildah-cmd param1 param2
container_linux.go:348: starting container process caused "exec: \"param1\": executable file not found in $PATH"

docker run buildah:cmd param1 param2
container_linux.go:247: starting container process caused "exec: \"param1\": executable file not found in $PATH"
/usr/bin/docker-current: Error response from daemon: oci runtime error: container_linux.go:247: starting container process caused "exec: \"param1\": executable file not found in $PATH".
~~~
Test issues are not related to this PR.  Looks like there's been a change to docker functionality that needs to be adjusted in .travis.yml.  Will spin up an issue get that fixed pronto.

Forgot to mention, I pulled the patch @pixdrift and it worked well for me, so TYVM for that.  If you could change the title of this PR so it's more descriptive, I'd appreciate it.  That way when we're scrolling through PR history months from now we'll more easily know what this addressed.
Test issues should be fixed with #572
@TomSweeneyRedHat thanks for fixing CI issues, title has been updated.

LGTM 
Once you fix your test errors.

I have fixed tests on the code, but still failing DCO. Attempted to submit a second pull request #576 with sign off in both commits but still get DCO errors. Can someone advise?

My DCO statements are in the commit comment as they were made through the GitHub UI. If I need to sign using git client i'll go back and do it again.

Try to do `git commit -s --amend` and then type `:sw` to go back to the terminal and run `git push -f origin patch-1`.

Hopefully this will fix the problem.

@ripcurld, cheers. I didn't have a git client handy when I was attempting to resolve the DCO issue so tried to fix it in the GitHub web UI.. which doesn't look possible at this stage.

Have one now, I will work through it.
Looks like DCO was happy in #577, happy to close this PR?
